--- title: "ex02. Unconditional DPM (SB Backend)" --- > **Legacy vignette (for the website / historical notes).** > These files may not match the current exported API one-to-one. > Last verified: **2026-01-18**. > > For the up-to-date workflow, see the main package vignettes (basic, unconditional, conditional, and causal).. # Unconditional DPmix: Stick-Breaking (SB) Backend **Purpose**: Showcase the stick-breaking backend that uses a fixed number of mixture components (`components = J`) and contrast it with the CRP backend from `start/backends-and-workflow`. In this vignette we fit **two** bulk kernels (**Gamma** and **Cauchy**) on the same dataset to highlight how heavier tails in the bulk can change the fit even without a GPD tail. --- ## Data Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Generate a reproducible mock dataset instead of relying on saved cache files. set.seed(10102) n <- 200 weights <- c(0.4, 0.35, 0.25) params <- list( list(shape = 2.0, rate = 1.5), list(shape = 4.0, rate = 1.0), list(shape = 7.0, rate = 0.7) ) component <- sample(seq_along(weights), size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights) y_mixed <- numeric(n) for (k in seq_along(params)) { idx <- which(component == k) if (length(idx) > 0) { y_mixed[idx] <- rgamma(length(idx), shape = params[[k]]$shape, rate = params[[k]]$rate) } } df_data <- data.frame(y = y_mixed) summary_tbl <- tibble( statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"), value = c(length(y_mixed), mean(y_mixed), sd(y_mixed), min(y_mixed), max(y_mixed)) ) p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) + geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.7, color = "black") + geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) + labs(title = "Raw Data: Mixed Gamma Distribution", x = "y", y = "Density") + theme_minimal() gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_raw, ncol = 1) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/data-setup-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Summary of the SB Dataset
statistic value
N 200.00
Mean 4.21
SD 4.11
Min 0.04
Max 19.60
````` ::: ::: --- ## Model Specification & Bundle We build the stick-breaking mixture explicitly with `components = 5` so that there is room for weight decay while keeping the MCMC runtime manageable for a vignette. We then refit the same SB model with a **Cauchy** kernel for comparison. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --- Gamma kernel --- bundle_sb_gamma <- DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle( y = y_mixed, kernel = "gamma", backend = "sb", components = 5, # fixed truncation level for SB GPD = FALSE, # bulk-only scenario mcmc = mcmc ) # --- Cauchy kernel --- bundle_sb_cauchy <- DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle( y = y_mixed, kernel = "cauchy", backend = "sb", components = 5, GPD = FALSE, mcmc = mcmc ) ``` ::: --- ## Running MCMC & Summary ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fit_sb_gamma <- load_or_fit("ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb-fit_sb_gamma", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb_gamma)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, scale, shape, w, z ===== Samplers ===== RW sampler (10) - alpha - shape[] (5 elements) - v[] (4 elements) conjugate sampler (5) - scale[] (5 elements) categorical sampler (200) - z[] (200 elements) ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} fit_sb_cauchy <- load_or_fit("ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb-fit_sb_cauchy", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb_cauchy)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, location, scale, w, z ===== Samplers ===== RW sampler (15) - alpha - location[] (5 elements) - scale[] (5 elements) - v[] (4 elements) categorical sampler (200) - z[] (200 elements) ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summary(fit_sb_gamma) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MixGPD summary | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Gamma Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE | epsilon: 0.025 n = 200 | components = 5 Summary Initial components: 5 | Components after truncation: 4 WAIC: 892.649 lppd: -370.921 | pWAIC: 75.403 Summary table parameter mean sd q0.025 q0.500 q0.975 ess weights[1] 0.367 0.065 0.254 0.365 0.49 3.667 weights[2] 0.272 0.042 0.2 0.265 0.366 19.811 weights[3] 0.176 0.04 0.102 0.18 0.248 16.275 weights[4] 0.113 0.036 0.05 0.11 0.186 8.979 alpha 2.139 1.021 0.843 1.973 3.973 27.87 shape[1] 2.34 0.685 1.468 2.227 3.663 13.214 shape[2] 2.787 0.712 1.468 2.796 4.045 26.75 shape[3] 2.494 0.905 1.139 2.398 4.376 50.539 shape[4] 2.026 1.431 0.745 1.536 6.539 29.379 scale[1] 1.174 0.471 0.223 1.138 2.12 37.248 scale[2] 0.601 0.55 0.223 0.426 2.278 10.835 scale[3] 0.531 0.493 0.216 0.383 1.852 24.976 scale[4] 1.619 1.465 0.233 1.097 6.025 23.898 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} summary(fit_sb_cauchy) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MixGPD summary | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Cauchy Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE | epsilon: 0.025 n = 200 | components = 5 Summary Initial components: 5 | Components after truncation: 5 WAIC: 1014.919 lppd: -323.213 | pWAIC: 184.246 Summary table parameter mean sd q0.025 q0.500 q0.975 ess weights[1] 0.321 0.037 0.249 0.32 0.398 22.991 weights[2] 0.253 0.031 0.19 0.255 0.305 14.877 weights[3] 0.181 0.03 0.125 0.18 0.235 17.309 weights[4] 0.137 0.024 0.094 0.14 0.185 13.26 weights[5] 0.107 0.024 0.065 0.105 0.161 10.173 alpha 1.922 0.826 0.536 1.832 3.448 74.526 location[1] 2.713 2.352 1.147 1.423 7.973 10.24 location[2] 4.474 2.734 1.045 5.369 8.542 26.664 location[3] 3.678 2.32 0.473 2.899 9.705 77.237 location[4] 3.795 3.394 0.389 3.183 10.268 28.141 location[5] 3.485 3.807 0.281 2.194 10.38 7.076 scale[1] 0.836 0.636 0.351 0.581 2.451 11.15 scale[2] 1.145 0.682 0.302 1.052 2.623 34.05 scale[3] 0.907 0.471 0.243 0.805 1.933 114.049 scale[4] 0.828 0.596 0.178 0.737 2.345 37.018 scale[5] 0.676 0.554 0.149 0.531 1.857 12.334 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} params_gamma <- params(fit_sb_gamma) params_gamma ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Posterior mean parameters $alpha [1] "2.139" $w [1] "0.367" "0.272" "0.176" "0.113" $shape [1] "2.34" "2.787" "2.494" "2.026" $scale [1] "1.174" "0.601" "0.531" "1.619" ``` ::: ::: --- ## MCMC Diagnostics (S3 Plot Methods) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Default diagnostics for each fit safe_plot(fit_sb_gamma, family = c("traceplot", "autocorrelation", "running")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-3.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(fit_sb_cauchy, family = c("density", "geweke", "caterpillar")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-4.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-5.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/diag-plots-6.png){width=768} ::: ::: Use `summary(fit_sb_gamma)` and `summary(fit_sb_cauchy)` to inspect effective sample size, R-hat, and other convergence diagnostics; the `plot()` calls above show the trace/density pairs for both global and stick-breaking weight parameters. --- ## Stick-Breaking Weights & Component Activity The stick-breaking weights are exposed via the `plot()` diagnostics above, and you can refer to the raw `fit_sb_gamma` / `fit_sb_cauchy` objects (or their `summary()` output) for posterior summaries of each component weight. --- ## Posterior Predictions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} y_grid <- seq(min(y_mixed), max(y_mixed) * 1.3, length.out = 250) pred_density_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, y = y_grid, type = "density") pred_density_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, y = y_grid, type = "density") safe_plot(pred_density_gamma) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/density-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(pred_density_cauchy) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/density-predict-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} quant_probs <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95) pred_q_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") pred_q_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") safe_plot(pred_q_gamma) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/quantile-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(pred_q_cauchy) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/quantile-predict-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pred_mean_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "mean") pred_mean_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, type = "mean") safe_plot(pred_mean_gamma) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/mean-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(pred_mean_cauchy) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/mean-predict-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Bulk-only vs CRP Backends ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} bundle_crp_small <- DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle( y = y_mixed, kernel = "gamma", backend = "crp", components = 5, GPD = FALSE, mcmc = mcmc ) fit_crp_small <- load_or_fit("ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb-fit_crp_small", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_crp_small)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, scale, shape, z ===== Samplers ===== CRP_concentration sampler (1) - alpha CRP_cluster_wrapper sampler (10) - scale[] (5 elements) - shape[] (5 elements) CRP sampler (1) - z[1:200] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [Warning] CRP_sampler: This MCMC is not for a proper model. The MCMC attempted to use more components than the number of cluster parameters. Please increase the number of cluster parameters. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} crp_quant <- predict(fit_crp_small, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") sb_quant <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") bind_rows( crp_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "CRP"), sb_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "SB") ) %>% select(any_of(c("model", "index", "estimate", "lower", "upper"))) %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>% kable(caption = "Quantile Comparison: CRP vs SB", align = "c") %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Quantile Comparison: CRP vs SB
model index estimate lower upper
CRP 0.05 0.214 0.089 0.393
CRP 0.25 0.600 0.332 0.926
CRP 0.50 1.121 0.672 1.871
CRP 0.75 2.522 1.197 6.487
CRP 0.95 5.354 2.300 13.074
SB 0.05 0.219 0.064 0.509
SB 0.25 0.692 0.299 1.178
SB 0.50 1.450 0.803 2.422
SB 0.75 3.146 1.623 7.170
SB 0.95 7.485 3.315 18.755
````` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(crp_quant) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/compare-plots-1.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(sb_quant) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex02-unconditional-dpm-sb_files/figure-html/compare-plots-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- For unconditional models, use `predict()` only; `fitted()` and `residuals()` are not supported. --- ## Takeaways - **SB Backend**: Fixed `components` keeps label-switching manageable, and the diagnostic plots via the S3 `plot()` method show weight dynamics. - **Kernels matter**: Gamma vs Cauchy can behave very differently in the shoulders/tails, even without a GPD tail. - **Predictions**: Posterior density, posterior-mean quantiles, and mean (via predictive sampling) are accessible via `predict()` with pointwise 95% credible intervals. - **Backend comparison**: The CRP fit (`start/backends-and-workflow`) and the SB-gamma fit deliver similar central quantiles while SB offers more control over truncation. - **Next**: Explore tail augmentation (`GPD = TRUE`) with the CRP backend in `ex03` or SB backend in `ex04`. --- ## Workflow Navigation - Previous: [ex01-unconditional-dpm-crp](ex01-unconditional-dpm-crp.html) - Next: [ex03-unconditional-dpmgpd-crp](ex03-unconditional-dpmgpd-crp.html) - Workflow index: [Roadmap](../roadmap.html) - Practical entry: [Examples](../examples/index.html)
